correctness.justification: All file paths, line numbers, and behavior descriptions verified against the code; one small slip — header.tsx is not a 'lone outlier' since view.tsx in the same directory also uses camelCase.

coverage.justification: Catches the high-impact issues (PostPage in /projects, ReportView tracker, ambiguous Page, incr route, link vs href, sorted mid-step name, Project type ambiguity) and picks up subtle ones like topic/interests and the error message phrasing.

prioritization.justification: Critical bucket (wrong domain, misleading name, ambiguous Page, implementation-leaking route) matches real reader-impact; medium and minor tiers are defensibly ordered.

actionability.justification: Every finding has a concrete replacement name, a specific file/line target, and usually an alternative — ready to mechanically apply.

rationale.justification: Each finding explains why it matters (searchability, convention alignment, implementation leakage, intent-naming) rather than asserting badness.

signal_density.justification: Compact numbered list with one tight paragraph per finding; no boilerplate, scaffolding, or repetition.

fabrications: #7 claims header.tsx is a 'lone outlier' for camelCase, but view.tsx in the same directory also uses camelCase — it's not alone.
overall_notes: Strong, tightly written audit: claims fact-check cleanly, priorities align with real reader impact, and each suggestion includes a concrete replacement name and location. Only nick is the 'lone outlier' overstatement for header.tsx. Otherwise a high-signal report with solid rationale throughout.
